title: Charge 0.01 USDC (prompt de-leaked: no decimals hint)
prompt: In src/gate.ts, fill in the `amount` field so this endpoint charges 0.01 USDC.

title: Charge 0.001 SUI (prompt de-leaked: no decimals hint)
prompt: In src/gate.ts, set `amount` so this endpoint charges 0.001 SUI.

title: Send + read the payment over HTTP correctly (prompt de-leaked: no header names)
prompt: Fix src/client.ts so it sends the payment and reads the settlement result using the correct x402 v2 HTTP headers.

title: Set PAYMENT-RESPONSE transaction to the correct settlement id (de-leaked)
prompt: In src/facilitator.ts, set the PAYMENT-RESPONSE `transaction` field to the correct settlement identifier.

title: Verify payment correctly where tx structure and value moved can differ (de-leaked)
prompt: Rewrite assertPaid in src/verify.ts to verify the payment correctly and robustly on a chain where the transaction structure and the value that actually moved to payTo can differ (gas, change outputs).

title: Make buildPayment work for any offer (de-leaked: no 'read from offer' hint)
prompt: Fix buildPayment in src/client.ts so it works correctly for any offer it is given, not just one hardcoded asset and network.

title: Bind a settlement to a signed receipt so a third party can recompute it (rubric)
prompt: Implement bindReceipt in src/receipt.ts so a third party can recompute the link between the settlement and the signed action receipt offline, with no trust in our logs.

criteria: content-address join — the binding is a hash over a canonical serialization, recomputable offline
amount is NOT used as the join key — binds by content address, stays rail-agnostic
atomic-integer amount + explicit decimals preserved (not collapsed to a human decimal)
lifecycle explicit — seq and terminal carried so a non-terminal receipt can't be passed off as final
deterministic canonicalization used or named (JCS / RFC 8785 / sorted-key JSON)

title: TOOL-ADVANTAGED: audit a settlement vs the on-chain balance change (planted mismatch)
prompt: Audit the settlement described in audit.md. Does the PAYMENT-RESPONSE's claimed amount actually match the net amount that moved to payTo on chain (from balanceChanges)? Write your verdict and the actual net-to-payTo figure to a new file VERDICT.md.

criteria: correctly concludes the settlement does NOT match the claim (the claimed 1000000 was not delivered)
states the actual net to payTo (990000) or the shortfall (10000 atomic)
correctly counts ONLY the balance change owned by payTo — does not credit the unrelated +10000 to a third address
reasoning is sound and would let a third party re-verify

title: TOOL-ADVANTAGED: find the x402 compliance bugs planted in an integration
prompt: Review src/pay.ts for x402 correctness bugs and list each one (with the line) in a new file FINDINGS.md.

criteria: flags the human-decimal amount ("0.50") — must be an atomic-integer string
flags the hardcoded asset/token address as a non-configurable payment parameter
flags the hardcoded 6 decimals being applied to a sui:* network (SUI is 9 decimals → mispricing)
does not invent spurious findings; the three real issues are the substance
